summary: Fair‑quality audience tape captures a stellar 1973 show highlighted by an epic Dark Star and strong mid‑set jams.
review: The performance is widely praised – especially the 31‑minute Dark Star, vibrant Cumberland Blues, Eyes, Playing in the Band and a gorgeous H.C. Sunshine – while the recording suffers from typical audience tape flaws (hiss, occasional whistle, slight pitch wobble). Even reviewers who docked points for sound agree the music makes this a must‑listen show.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the musical performance very highly (many 5‑star comments) while noting moderate to fair audio quality; the weighted blend yields a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Typical audience tape with some hiss, occasional whistle from the crowd and a slight pitch rise in Box of Rain. Instruments are generally clear but the mix is uneven, making parts sound weak.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A solid opening set moves into a marathon Dark Star that dominates the second half, followed by tight mid‑set jams (Eyes → Playing in the Band). The flow is praised despite the audio imperfections.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar work on Dark Star is highlighted – some note arpeggio exercises near the end but overall tone and improvisation are lauded.
